data.title: Nebius stock surges 20% after Q2 results show huge revenue jump, with raised guidance._NVDA-backed cloud provider.
data.description: The stock of Nebius surged by almost 20% following a significant increase in revenue reported by the cloud provider, backed by Nvidia. In the second quarter, Nebius Group saw a substantial boost in its financial performance. This positive development led to a surge in its stock price, reflecting investor confidence. The company also revised its guidance upwards, indicating strong prospects for future growth.

data.title: Firefly Aerospace ups IPO; debut on Thursday. Rocket Lab, Kratos Defense down pre-earnings.
data.description: Firefly Aerospace is set to make its debut on Thursday with an increased initial public offering (IPO). Rocket Lab and Kratos Defense are both experiencing declines in anticipation of their upcoming earnings reports. Firefly Aerospace's IPO has been upsized, creating buzz in the market. Rocket Lab is seeing a slight ease in its performance, while investors eagerly await its financial results. The latest developments point to a dynamic and competitive environment in the aerospace industry.

data.title: Billionaire Robert Rosenkranz finds wisdom in ancient Stoics; seeks to apply in modern life.
data.description: Robert Rosenkranz, a billionaire known for his unique perspective, draws inspiration from the ancient Stoics for guidance in his life. Despite his wealth and success, Rosenkranz's philosophy is rooted in principles that he believes offer timeless wisdom. His approach contrasts with the traditional views of many in his position, making him stand out in the business world. The influence of Stoic teachings on Rosenkranz's decisions and mindset reflects his commitment to finding innovative and meaningful ways to navigate contemporary challenges.

data.title: Fortinet's Q2 results meet expectations, but stock falls; confusion over firewall refresh. Cybersecurity sector sells off.
data.description: Fortinet's stock saw a surge after their Q2 earnings report, with revenue and billings coming in slightly above analyst estimates. However, the June quarter guidance was in line with expectations. Despite this positive news, the stock fell on confusion among investors regarding the firewall refresh. This led to a sell-off in cybersecurity stocks.

data.title: Intel stock falls after Trump says CEO Lip-Bu Tan "must resign" due to conflict of interest.
data.description: Intel's shares experienced a decline following President Trump's statement that CEO Lip-Bu Tan "must resign," citing him as "highly conflicted." The stock saw a drop in response to this development.

data.title: Caterpillar stock downgrade drags Dow; Nasdaq maintains gains. Dow Jones impacted, Nasdaq holds steady.
data.description: The stock of Caterpillar experienced a decline following a downgrade, which had a negative impact on the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The Dow was affected by this drop, while the Nasdaq managed to maintain a positive gain. The development was covered in a live broadcast titled "Stock Market Today: Dow Weighed Down By Caterpillar; Nasdaq Holds A Gain, For Now."

data.title: Apple stock rises 3% with $100B investment. Eli Lilly crashes 14% due to drug trial results which caused $100B loss. Market dips overall.
data.description: U.S. equities experienced fluctuations due to corporate earnings and new tariff announcements by President Trump. The S&P 500 fell by 0.3% to 6,328, the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped by 0.8% to 43,865, and the Nasdaq 100 remained nearly unchanged at 23,307. Apple Inc. (AAPL) saw a 3% increase after gaining 5.1% the previous day, with investors optimistic about the company avoiding new import duties. Apple committed to investing an additional $100 billion in U.S. manufacturing. Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) had a significant downturn of 14%, attributed to its experimental weight-loss pill, which yielded a 12.4% weight loss, below analyst expectations. This led to a market capitalization loss of over $100 billion. More details on this news available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Peloton beats Q4 EPS estimate at 5 cents, revenue at $606.90 million, down 6% YoY. Stock falls. Paid subscriptions at 2.80M, down 6% YoY.
data.description: Peloton Interactive (NASDAQ: PTON) released its quarterly earnings report on Thursday. In the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025, the company posted earnings per share of $0.05, surpassing the analyst consensus of a $0.06 loss. Despite this, PTON stock is currently experiencing a decline. The fitness-equipment maker achieved quarterly sales of $606.90 million, which is a 6% decrease from the previous year but exceeds the expected $580.24 million. Furthermore, the Connected Fitness Products Revenue dropped by $13.5 million, a 6% decline year-over-year due to reduced sales and deliveries offset by a shift towards higher-priced products. The Ending Paid Connected Fitness Subscriptions for the quarter were reported at 2.80 million, reflecting a 6% decrease year-over-year. Subscription Revenue saw a decrease of $23.2 million.

data.title: Martin Marietta Materials reports Q2 2025 revenue at $1.81B, up 3% YoY. Net earnings up 12% to $328M, EPS $5.43. EBITDA up 8% to $630M, margin at 34.8%, gross profit at $544M. Aggregates revenue rose 6% to $1.32B, gross profit up 9% to $430M. Cement and concrete revenue fell 6% to $245M, gross profit down 25% to $54M.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, Martin Marietta Materials, Inc. (NYSE: MLM) posted a revenue of $1.811 billion, marking a 3% increase compared to the previous year but falling short of the analyst estimate of $1.896 billion. The company's net earnings saw a 12% growth, reaching $328 million, with diluted earnings per share hitting $5.43, surpassing the estimated $5.35. Adjusted EBITDA rose by 8% to $630 million, and the margin widened to 34.8%, a 168 basis points increase. Gross profit also experienced a 5% boost, reaching $544 million.Within the Building Materials segment, revenue totaled $1.721 billion, up by 2%, while gross profit surged by 3% to $517 million. Aggregates revenue saw a 6% increase to $1.32 billion, driven by a 7% uptick in the average selling price to $23.21 per ton despite a 1% decrease in shipments. Gross profit for Aggregates climbed 9% to $430 million, leading to a margin expansion to 33%. On the other hand, revenue for Cement and ready mixed concrete dropped by 6% to $245 million, with gross profit declining by 25% to $54 million. Asphalt and paving figures were not provided in this excerpt.
